DISCOVERY, 301, PLAINTIFF DESIRAE FERIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL MATT WAYNE, KIM KELLY, CHRISTINE LEE, AND DANIELLE HOUCK TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND/OR ITS COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $8,560.00 IS DENIED. AS TO KIMBERLY KELLY, THE MOTION IS MOOT. AS TO THE REMAINING INDIVIDUALS, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT SHE LACKS OTHER MEANS TO OBTAIN THE INFORMATION SHE SEEKS, THAT THE INFORMATION SHE SEEKS IS CRUCIAL TO HER CASE, AND/OR THAT THE INFORMATION SOUGHT IS RELEVANT AND NOT PRIVILEGED. THE OPPOSING PARTY IS TO PRESENT A PROPOSED ORDER FOR THE COURT'S REVIEW CONSISTENT WITH THIS RULING. PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED. VISITING JUDGE: JOHN M. TRUE; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/JMT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-05-2026 Continued To Sep-08-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant San Francisco Unified School District To Provide Further Responses, Without Objection, And To Produce Documents Responsive To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set Seven)
Off Calendar May-22-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF DESIRAE FERIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, AND TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (SET SEVEN), NOS. 143-167; OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS; AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS (C.C.P. 2031.310, 2023.010, 2023.030)

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Desirae Feria's motion to compel defendant San Francisco Unified School District to make further responses to her requests for production nos. 143-167, for production, and for monetary, issue, and evidentiary sanctions is denied. Feria's motion lacks substantial justification and the court orders her to pay $855 in sanctions to SFUSD.

The RFPs at issue seek work product; they ask for all documents that SFUSD intends to use to impeach Feria and her witnesses at trial. An attorney's curation of which documents in the case they will use for impeachment is derivative, reflects the attorney's deliberations and impressions, and is entitled to absolute work product protection. (See Coito v. Superior Court (2012) 54 Cal.4th 480, 495.) The requests at issue here are more invasive of counsel's preparation than those at issue in City of Long Beach v. Superior Court (1976) 64 Cal.App.3d 65, which merely asked for a list of trial witnesses and did not invade the content of counsel's planned cross-examination.

In addition, Feria's motion was untimely noticed. A motion must be noticed with sixteen court days plus time for service. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1005, subd. (b); id., sec. 1010.6, subd. (a)(3)(B) [electronic service extends "[a]ny period of notice" by two days, in addition to extending "any . . . duty to . . . make any response"].)

In addition, Feria's motion seeks issue and evidentiary sanctions without showing that SFUSD has failed to comply with a discovery order. Ordinarily, "the discovery statutes evince an incremental approach to discovery sanctions . . . ." (Creed-21 v. City of Wildomar (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 690, 701.) It is generally unwarranted for a court to impose an issue or evidentiary sanction as first recourse.

Feria's discovery position lacks substantial justification and SFUSD's requested sanctions are reasonable. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.310, subd. (h).) Feria shall pay $855 in sanctions to SFUSD.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant San Francisco Unified School District To Provide Further Responses, Without Objection, To Special Interrogatories (Set Seven)
Off Calendar May-22-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF DESIRAE FERIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET SEVEN) (C.C.P. 2030.300)

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Desirae Feria's motion to compel defendant San Francisco Unified School District to make further responses to her special interrogatories, set seven, and for monetary, issue, and evidentiary sanctions is denied. Feria's motion lacks substantial justification and the court orders her to pay $855 in sanctions to SFUSD.

The special interrogatories at issue here ask for the facts that counsel intends to use at trial to impeach various witnesses. For the reasons discussed in the court's companion order on Feria's motion to compel further responses to similar requests for production, this discovery seeks material protected by absolute work product production.

In addition, Feria's motion was untimely noticed. A motion must be noticed with sixteen court days plus time for service. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1005, subd. (b); id., sec. 1010.6, subd. (a)(3)(B) [electronic service extends "[a]ny period of notice" by two days, in addition to extending "any . . . duty to . . . make any response"].)

In addition, Feria's motion seeks issue and evidentiary sanctions without showing that SFUSD has failed to comply with a discovery order. Ordinarily, "the discovery statutes evince an incremental approach to discovery sanctions . . . ." (Creed-21 v. City of Wildomar (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 690, 701.) It is generally unwarranted for a court to impose an issue or evidentiary sanction as first recourse.

Feria's discovery position lacks substantial justification. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2030.300, subd. (d).) The court concludes that $855 is a reasonable sanction amount incurred by SFUSD in opposing this motion. Feria shall pay $855 in sanctions to SFUSD.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: denied as to interrogatories 212 and 217 and granted as to interrogatories 218-221. The contact information for current SFUSD employees is not discoverable because those employees may be contacted through defense counsel. The requested information for the four persons who reported disabilities to SFUSD who were supervised directly or indirectly by Michelle Zapata is discoverable because those persons are within the possible persons who have discoverable "me too" information. SFUSD must serve further verified responses consistent with this order no later than May 13, 2026. Both sides' requests for monetary sanctions are denied. Both sides acted with substantial justification regarding the issues raised by this motion. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-05-2025 Set For Trial On Feb-23-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-30-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. AARON GBEWONYO, Esq. of Shegerian & Associates, Inc. (tel: 310-860-0770) for Plaintiff.
Atty. COREY WILSON, Esq. of Bertrand, Fox, Elliot, Osman & Wenzel (tel: 415-353-0999) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, AND MICHELLE ZAPATA's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants San Francisco Unified School District and Michelle Zapata move to strike punitive damages allegations from plaintiff Desirae Feria's First Amended Complaint (FAC). For the reasons stated herein the court denies the motion.

The court previously granted the motion to strike. Feria added allegations, notably the allegations that her supervisor Zapata forced her to work as a substitute in violation of her work restrictions, which exacerbated her injury and forced her to begin using crutches. (FAC para. 14.e.) The FAC further alleges that Zapata was aware of Feria's work restrictions but disregarded them in order to cause Feria discomfort and drive her to quit. (Id.)

"In order to state a prima facie claim for punitive damages, a complaint must set forth the elements as stated in the general punitive damage statute, Civil Code section 3294. These statutory elements include allegations that the defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud or malice. 'Malice' is defined in the statute as conduct 'intended by the defendant to cause injury to plaintiff, or despicable conduct that is carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights or safety of others.' 'Oppression' means despicable conduct that subjects a person to cruel and unjust hardship in conscious disregard of that person's rights.' " (Turman v. Turning Point of Central California, Inc. (2010) 191 Cal.App.4th 53, 63 [internal citations and quotation marks omitted].) The court concludes that Feria has now adequately pled the basis for punitive damages in Zapata's alleged knowledge of her injury and intent to exacerbate her injury.

The motion to strike is accordingly denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing RE: DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, AND MICHELLE ZAPATA's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants San Francisco Unified School District and Michelle Zapata's motion to strike portions of Plaintiff Desirae Feria's complaint is granted.

Feria opposes only the request to strike punitive damages allegations against Zapata. These allegations, however, fall short of showing fraud, oppression, or malice. Feria has leave to amend her complaint to allege facts supporting a punitive damages request.

Feria's first amended complaint must be filed and served by February 25, 2025.

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
